mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which depress corporate earnings expectations and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing activity, which reduces corporate profitability and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts signals stronger consumer demand and business investment, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where HOUST increases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP and industrial production indicates robust economic expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings visibility.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of instances where both GDPC1 and INDPRO grow.

mechanism_narrative: During high inflation regimes, rising federal funds rates signal tighter monetary policy that curbs equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll gains below 100k signal weak job market performance, which reduces consumer spending confidence and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS < 100k.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) and GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates weakening economic demand, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where both RSAFS and GDPC1 decline.
